mnemonics: CLASH for Session Tracking: Cookies, URL Rewriting, Hidden Form Fields, HTTPSession, HttpServletResponSe.setHeader
keyPoints: Session tracking maintains user state across multiple HTTP requests.
JSP (JavaServer Pages) simplifies dynamic web content creation by allowing Java code in HTML pages.
JSP elements include directives (<%@ %>), declarations (<%! %>), scriptlets (<% %>), and expressions (<%= %>).
commonMistakes: Forgetting to call session.invalidate() when logging out, causing security risks.
Mixing business logic (Java code) with presentation (HTML) in JSPs, violating MVC principles.
Not handling the case where cookies are disabled, breaking session tracking that relies solely on them.
